pain.pain: “I’ve been freelancing as a web developer for 2 years and my income is all over the place. One month I make $6k, the next month I’m scrambling to make $1k. I feel like I’m always ‘busy’ but not actually building anything predictable or stable.”

“I have no idea how to find good clients consistently. I rely on word-of-mouth and random job boards. I send tons of proposals and either never hear back or get ghosted after the first call. It’s draining and makes me question if I’m even good at what I do.”

“When clients ask for my rates, I freeze. I either undercharge because I’m afraid they’ll say no, or I quote something higher and then feel guilty the whole time. I don’t have a clear pricing structure and I’m terrified of hearing ‘you’re too expensive.’”

“I’m stuck doing low-level implementation work instead of higher-value strategy. Clients see me as ‘the person who builds the thing’ instead of a partner. I know I could charge more if I positioned myself better, but I don’t know how to make that shift.”

“Every project feels like reinventing the wheel. I don’t have a clear process, timelines always slip, and I end up working nights and weekends to hit deadlines. Scope creep is constant, but I feel bad pushing back because I’m scared of losing the client.”

“I want to hit $8–10k/month consistently without burning out. Right now I’m saying yes to every project, even the ones that are a bad fit, because I’m scared to turn down money. I don’t know how to plan my pipeline or forecast my income.”

“I’m overwhelmed by all the advice online. Some people say ‘niche down,’ others say ‘just get more clients,’ and others talk about funnels and email lists. I just want a straightforward, step-by-step path that I can follow to build a steady, respectable freelance business.”

“Sales calls make me super anxious. I either talk too much and overshare technical details, or I under-explain and the client doesn’t see the value. I don’t have a script or a structure, so every call feels like I’m winging it.”

“I struggle with boundaries. Clients message me at all hours, expect instant responses, and slip extra tasks into the project. I want to be ‘nice’ and helpful, but it ends up eating my weekends and evenings. I feel resentful and exhausted by the end of each project.”

“I’ve bought a bunch of generic business courses, but they’re usually geared toward coaches or agencies. I want something made specifically for solo freelance developers who work with clients one-on-one and want to grow to a stable, sustainable income without building a huge team.”

offer.pain: Income instability and feast-or-famine months as a freelance web developer
offer.offer: Core video training: "From Feast-or-Famine to Fully Booked: The Freelance Dev Income System" – 8-module, step-by-step video course walking through positioning, lead generation, sales, pricing, and delivery systems specifically for freelance web developers. Includes screen-share demos of real outreach, proposal creation, call breakdowns, and project dashboards. Estimated value: $699.

offer.pain: Struggling to find and convert good clients consistently
offer.offer: Client Pipeline Playbook – a detailed, actionable system for generating and tracking leads from 3 proven channels (warm network, targeted cold outreach, and curated job boards). Includes templates for outreach emails, follow-up sequences, and a Notion/Google Sheets pipeline tracker. Estimated value: $249.

offer.pain: Anxiety and uncertainty around pricing and quoting projects
offer.offer: Freelance Pricing Toolkit – a set of calculators, pricing models, and plug-and-play proposal templates for fixed-price, hourly, and retainer projects. Includes a rate calculator, sample proposals with real numbers, and a "Price with Confidence" mini-training. Estimated value: $197.

offer.pain: Being seen as a low-level implementer instead of a strategic partner
offer.offer: Positioning & Offer Design Workshop – training on crafting a clear, outcome-focused offer (e.g., "I help local service businesses turn their website into a lead machine") with examples tailored to different dev stacks (WordPress, Webflow, React, etc.). Includes positioning statement swipe file and before/after website copy examples. Estimated value: $179.

offer.pain: Chaotic projects, scope creep, and poor boundaries
offer.offer: Project Delivery & Boundaries System – plug-and-play onboarding questionnaire, scope of work templates, change request form, communication guidelines, and project timeline templates. Includes email scripts for pushing back on scope creep and setting expectations from day one. Estimated value: $229.

offer.pain: Unpredictable income and lack of planning for $8–10k/month
offer.offer: Revenue Planning & Capacity Planner – spreadsheet and walkthrough training that helps freelancers map their target income, realistic capacity (hours per week), and project mix (builds vs. retainers) for the next 3–6 months. Includes sample "$8k/month" and "$10k/month" scenarios. Estimated value: $149.

offer.pain: Overwhelm from conflicting advice and lack of a clear path
offer.offer: 90-Day Roadmap for Freelance Devs – a day-by-day and week-by-week action plan that spells out what to do first, second, and third, with realistic time estimates for someone working 20–30 billable hours/week. Includes printable checklist and progress tracker. Estimated value: $129.

offer.pain: Sales call anxiety and lack of a repeatable call structure
offer.offer: Sales Call Scripts & Recording Breakdown – proven call script frameworks for discovery and proposal review calls, plus 3 anonymized real call recordings with commentary on what to say, what to avoid, and how to handle objections. Includes a one-page "Call Confidence" cheat sheet. Estimated value: $179.

offer.pain: Weak boundaries, constant messages, and burnout risk
offer.offer: Client Communication & Boundaries Pack – pre-written onboarding email, "how we work" one-pager, offboarding checklist, and scripts for setting response times, handling weekend requests, and turning extra work into paid change orders. Estimated value: $129.

offer.pain: Need for dev-specific, practical guidance (not generic business advice)
offer.offer: Freelance Dev Resource Library – curated scripts, examples, and templates specifically for web developers (e.g., sample GitHub portfolio structure, Loom walkthrough template, dev-focused case study examples, and a "tech stack translation" guide for non-technical clients). Estimated value: $199.

offer.pain: Desire for accountability and support while implementing
offer.offer: Private Community & Live Q&A Calls (8 weeks) – access to a private Slack/Discord group with other freelance developers, plus weekly live Q&A/co-working calls for 8 weeks to get feedback on positioning, proposals, and client situations. Estimated value: $299.

offer.pain: Fear of investing without seeing if the system works for them
offer.offer: Quick-Win Implementation Sprint – a 7-day guided challenge to land at least 3 new qualified conversations with potential clients using the provided outreach templates, so they see momentum within the first week. Includes daily prompts and check-ins. Estimated value: $99.

outline.outline:
Course Name & Promise:

Working Title: "Booked Solid Dev: A Practical System to Hit $8–10k/Month as a Freelance Web Developer"

Subheading / Promise: "Go from feast-or-famine freelancing to a steady pipeline of well-paying clients in 90 days, without sleazy sales tactics, paid ads, or building a big agency."

Ideal Student Persona:
- Name: Alex Ramirez
- Age: 28–38
- Background: Self-taught or bootcamp-trained web developer, 1–3 years freelancing or doing side projects
- Current Income: $2k–$6k/month inconsistent
- Goal: Stable $8–10k/month as a solo or lean freelance dev, working with 4–8 good clients per year
- Tech Stack: JavaScript, React, Next.js or WordPress/Webflow; comfortable building small business sites or simple apps
- Biggest Struggles: Inconsistent leads, undercharging, weak positioning, chaotic projects, poor boundaries

Transformation Statement:
"This course takes you from anxious, underpaid freelance developer who lives from project to project, to a confident, well-positioned professional with a repeatable system for attracting, closing, and delivering high-value client projects at stable, predictable income levels."

Module 1 – Foundations: Designing Your Freelance Dev Business

Goal: Get crystal clear on your income targets, strengths, and constraints, and design a freelance business model that fits your life instead of copying random advice from the internet.

Key Lessons:
1. Your Why, Your Numbers: Defining Success Beyond "More Clients"
   - Exercise: Map your real monthly expenses, desired savings, and lifestyle costs
   - Convert that into a clear income target and minimum acceptable rate
2. Picking a Profitable Service Focus (Without Over-Niching)
   - Identify 1–2 core services (e.g., "conversion-focused landing pages" or "website redesigns for local service businesses")
   - Evaluate markets based on budget, urgency, and ease of access
3. Positioning Yourself as a Problem Solver, Not a Coder-for-Hire
   - Craft a positioning statement that passes the "coffee shop test"
   - Before/after examples of weak vs. strong positioning
4. Your Offer Stack: Turning Skills into a Clear, Sellable Package
   - Turn your tech skills into outcomes clients care about
   - Decide what’s included, what’s extra, and what’s off-limits

Deliverables by end of Module 1:
- Written income targets and minimum acceptable rate
- Draft positioning statement
- Defined core offer(s) with scope rough draft

Module 2 – Creating a Client-Attracting Portfolio & Case Studies

Goal: Build lean but compelling proof that you can get results, even if you don’t have many past clients.

Key Lessons:
1. What Clients Actually Look For in a Developer Portfolio
   - Debunking myths about fancy designs and huge case studies
   - The 3 questions a client wants answered in 10 seconds
2. Crafting Outcome-Focused Case Studies
   - Structure: Problem → Process → Result → Social Proof
   - Example case studies for small businesses, SaaS, and content sites
3. Building Fast, Focused Portfolio Pieces (Even If You’re New)
   - How to use past work, side projects, and collaborations as case studies
   - When and how to do 1–2 strategic discounted/"beta" projects
4. Website & Profile Tune-Up
   - Turning your website from an online resume into a lead-converting asset
   - Updating LinkedIn, GitHub, and other profiles to align with your positioning

Deliverables by end of Module 2:
- 2–3 outcome-focused case studies (client or project-based)
- Updated simple portfolio site or landing page
- Aligned LinkedIn and GitHub profiles

Module 3 – Lead Generation: Building a Simple, Repeatable Client Pipeline

Goal: Stop relying on luck and random referrals by setting up 2–3 reliable, low-stress lead sources.

Key Lessons:
1. The 3 Proven Lead Channels for Freelance Devs (Without Ads)
   - Warm network outreach
   - Targeted cold outreach to businesses you can genuinely help
   - Curated job boards and communities (and how to stand out)
2. Warm Outreach Without Feeling Salesy
   - How to re-activate your existing network (coworkers, classmates, past clients)
   - Message templates and follow-up sequences
3. Cold Outreach That Gets Replies
   - How to research and qualify potential clients quickly
   - Writing short, respectful emails that focus on value, not begging for work
   - Setting realistic outreach volume and tracking it
4. Smart Use of Job Boards & Platforms
   - How to pick the 1–2 platforms that make sense for your niche
   - Proposal frameworks that stand out without racing to the bottom on price
5. Tracking, Measuring, and Optimizing Your Pipeline
   - Simple spreadsheet or Notion template to track leads, calls, and conversion rate
   - Weekly review routine to see what’s working

Deliverables by end of Module 3:
- 90-day lead generation plan
- 3–5 warm outreach messages sent
- 20+ cold outreach or job board applications using the templates
- Simple pipeline tracker set up and in use

Module 4 – Confident Sales: From First Message to Signed Contract

Goal: Turn interested leads into well-scoped, fairly priced projects using a simple, repeatable sales process.

Key Lessons:
1. Qualifying Leads Quickly (So You Don’t Waste Hours on Bad Fits)
   - 5 key questions to ask before booking a call
   - Red flags and when to politely walk away
2. Structuring a Discovery Call That Builds Trust
   - 3-part call framework: context, diagnosis, prescription
   - Live/demo example of a real discovery call
3. Talking About Money Without Awkwardness
   - Language to anchor value before you mention price
   - How to handle "that’s too expensive" and "can you do it cheaper?"
4. Writing Proposals That Close (Without Being 20 Pages Long)
   - Simple 3–5 page proposal structure
   - Real-world proposal examples with annotated commentary
5. Getting the Yes: Contracts, Deposits, and Start Dates
   - How to ask for a deposit confidently
   - Simple contract essentials (with template you can customize)

Deliverables by end of Module 4:
- Discovery call script customized to your services
- Proposal template ready to reuse
- At least 1–2 real proposals sent using the system

Module 5 – Delivery Systems: Scope, Boundaries, and Smooth Projects

Goal: Deliver high-quality work without burning out, using clear processes and boundaries that clients respect.

Key Lessons:
1. Scoping Projects So They Don’t Spiral
   - How to define what’s included, what’s not, and what’s a separate phase
   - Translating technical work into client-understandable milestones
2. Onboarding Clients Like a Pro
   - Kickoff call agenda
   - Onboarding questionnaire and asset checklist
3. Managing Communication & Expectations
   - Setting communication norms (response times, tools, and boundaries)
   - Updates cadence that keeps clients calm and confident
4. Handling Scope Creep and Change Requests
   - Phrases and email scripts to gently but firmly redirect back to scope
   - Turning change requests into paid add-ons
5. Offboarding and Turning Projects into Long-Term Relationships
   - Handover documentation templates
   - Asking for testimonials and referrals without being awkward

Deliverables by end of Module 5:
- Reusable onboarding & offboarding checklists
- Written communication guidelines you share with clients
- Change request process and scripts

Module 6 – Pricing, Planning, and Hitting $8–10k/Month Sustainably

Goal: Build a realistic plan to reach and maintain $8–10k/month as a solo freelance dev without working 80-hour weeks.

Key Lessons:
1. Understanding Your True Hourly Rate (and Why It’s Higher Than You Think)
   - Factoring in admin time, marketing, and downtime
   - Avoiding the "race to the bottom" trap
2. Choosing a Pricing Model That Fits Your Services
   - When to use fixed-price, hourly, or retainers
   - Hybrid models and phased projects
3. Building Your $8–10k/Month Plan
   - Example project mixes (e.g., 2 builds + 3 retainers)
   - Capacity planning: how many active projects at once is realistic
4. Creating a 90-Day Execution Plan
   - Translating goals into weekly activities
   - Setting minimum marketing non-negotiables
5. Systems for Consistency
   - Weekly CEO hour routine
   - Simple dashboards to track revenue, leads, and close rate

Deliverables by end of Module 6:
- Personalized income and capacity plan
- Chosen pricing model and rate guidelines
- 90-day execution plan on paper or in your preferred tool

Bonuses & Support

Bonus 1: Templates & Scripts Vault
- All email templates (outreach, follow-ups, scope creep responses, testimonial requests)
- Proposal templates, contract starter, onboarding/offboarding docs

Bonus 2: Quick-Win Client Sprint (7 Days)
- Daily prompts and scripts to land at least 3 qualified conversations in a week
- Recommended daily schedule (1–2 hours) for those still working a day job

Bonus 3: Live Q&A & Hotseat Calls (8 Weeks)
- Weekly group calls to review positioning, outreach messages, and proposals
- Recorded and timestamped for later viewing

Community Access:
- Private Slack/Discord group for 3 months
- Channels for: Wins, Outreach Accountability, Proposal Feedback, Tech Questions

Guarantee:
- 30-day "Do the Work" Guarantee: If you complete the first 3 modules, send at least 20 outreach messages using the templates, and don’t see any increase in conversations or clear next steps, you can request a full refund within 30 days of purchase.

Course Sales Page Draft (Summary)

Headline:
"Booked Solid Dev: A Practical System to Hit $8–10k/Month as a Freelance Web Developer in 90 Days"

Subheadline:
"Stop guessing, undercharging, and waiting for referrals. Build a simple, repeatable system to attract great clients, close well-scoped projects, and deliver work you’re proud of—without burning out."

Who This Is For:
- Freelance web developers earning between $2k–$6k/month inconsistently
- Developers who have basic technical skills but struggle with business, sales, and boundaries
- Devs who want to stay solo or small (no desire to build a big agency)

What You’ll Learn:
- How to position yourself as a problem-solving expert, not a commodity coder
- How to create lean but powerful case studies that impress clients
- How to build a reliable lead generation system using warm outreach, cold outreach, and curated job boards
- How to run low-stress, effective sales calls and send proposals that win
- How to set boundaries, prevent scope creep, and manage projects smoothly
- How to price your work and plan your capacity to reach $8–10k/month sustainably

What’s Included:
- 6 core modules with step-by-step video lessons
- Downloadable templates, scripts, and checklists
- 90-day action roadmap
- 7-day Quick-Win Client Sprint
- 8 weeks of live group Q&A calls
- 3 months of private community access

Results You Can Expect (with effort):
- A clear, confident answer when someone asks "What do you do?"
- A portfolio and a couple of case studies that you’re proud to send to prospects
- A simple system you can follow weekly to generate leads
- Higher close rates on proposals and fewer price objections
- Projects that feel calmer, more predictable, and more profitable

Call to Action:
- Primary: "Enroll Now"
- Secondary: "See the Full Curriculum"

Pricing (example positioning, can be adjusted):
- One-time payment: $697
- Or 3 monthly payments of $259

Risk Reversal:
"Try Booked Solid Dev for 30 days. If you complete the first 3 modules and send at least 20 outreach messages using the included templates and you still don’t feel more confident about your pipeline and next steps, email us for a full refund. No hard feelings."
